title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted overflow-safe reference-counted graph, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in overflow-safe reference-counted graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Retain Release Invariant
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 6941. Produce bounded C evidence to model strong counts and expose a cycle that cannot reach zero; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.

C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Threaded Refcount Cost, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Making the count atomic makes the whole referenced object thread-safe.
